title: gunnera tinctoria var. meyeri
intro: Gunnera tinctoria var. meyeri, commonly known as the New Zealand daisy or parsnip, is a variety of the Gunnera tinctoria plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the gunnera family. The plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly for its roots, which contain a yellow pigment that can be used as a dye. The roots of the plant have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
